diagrams.title: Virtual Address Translation in Paging
diagrams.description: Shows how virtual address is divided into page number and offset, then translated using page table to physical address
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how paging translates virtual addresses to physical addresses using page tables and how offset remains the same during translation.

diagrams.title: Page Fault Handling Process
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing steps when a page fault occurs in demand paging system
elements: Check if page in memory (TLB/Page Table)
Page Fault occurs if not in memory
Page loaded from disk to frame
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the complete sequence of events when a page fault occurs and how the operating system handles it.

diagrams.title: Working Set Model
diagrams.description: Graph showing relationship between number of frames allocated and page fault rate, illustrating thrashing zone
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how the working set model helps determine optimal frame allocation and identify thrashing conditions.

diagrams.title: Two-Level Page Table Structure
diagrams.description: Shows hierarchical page table organization for large address spaces
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how multi-level page tables reduce memory overhead by only allocating page tables for needed virtual memory regions.
